---
title: "Managing A Process"
---

## Start a Process

To start a process,

1. Navigate to Process Management > Tasks > My Tasks and click Start Process.
2. Click the name of the process created by you in Studio Modeler. Example: Document AI Process.
3. The start event for our process was a created upload form, so go ahead and Please note: You can upload files from your local storage or from the attached content repository.

## Upload a Document

The IDP process configured by you in the previous section had a form to upload documents. You can upload files that you can process using the IDP classification and extraction services You can upload files from your local storage or from the attached content repository.

To upload a document:

1. On the Processes page, click Attach. The Select a File dialog box is displayed.
2. Select the check box next to the relevant files from the Repository or the Local Storage.
3. Click Start Process. The process is started and a confirmation message is displayed.

Note: To track the progress of the job, navigate to Studio Admin > Process Admin >  Process Instances, click the ellipsis button next to the process created by you and select Diagram. The Process Diagram page shows the current state of the process.
## Verifying Classification Results

The tasks assigned to you appear in your workspace. You can adjust and verify classification results. You can also reject or delete documents from the batch.

To verify classification results:

1. Navigate to Workspace >  Process Management  > My Tasks to view a list of tasks assigned to you.
2. Select the task to open Classification review. The results displayed here are based on the documents uploaded.

Note:
- You can also flag or delete documents from the batch.
- You can cut, movc and insert a page from its original location to a new location within the batch.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
## Flag a Document

During verification you can flag documents for exception handling from the batch review and document review interfaces. You can select a justificaton reason from the list configured by the solution builder along with a note that will follow the flagged document through the remainder of processing.

You can also re-open the flag document dialog box to view and edit the flag reason and note. Additionally, you can choose to remove the flag

![](data:image/png;base64,)
